about.title: Short Bio
about.bio: Stefano Giacomelli (Graduate Student Member, IEEE) was born in Rieti, Italy, in 1992. He received the Diploma in Electric Guitar from the Saint Louis College of Music, Rome, in 2018, the B.A. degree (cum laude) in Electroacoustic Music Composition in 2020, and the M.A. degree (cum laude, merit mention) in Sound Direction and Technologies in 2023, both from the "A. Casella" Conservatory of Music, L'Aquila. Since 2023, he has been pursuing the Ph.D. degree in Information and Communication Technologies with the Department of Information Engineering, Computer Science, and Mathematics (DISIM), University of L'Aquila. His research interests include Digital Signal Processing (DSP), Music Information Retrieval (MIR), and Computational Auditory Scene Analysis (CASA), with a focus on deep learning-based signal embedding and neural network optimization, for which he has co-authored several papers.
about.bio2: He actively collaborates on national and European interdisciplinary research projects bridging scientific and artistic domains — including musical extended realities (PRIN - Musical Metaverse 2022) and artistic/musicological research methodologies (EAR - Enacting Artistic Research WP3). He is also a composer, sound director, performer, and software developer for contemporary acoustic and electroacoustic productions. S. Giacomelli is the recipient of several awards, including national prizes and merit mentions at the Italian National Arts Award (MUR).
